risk_if_not_implemented: Without Password Managers, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Authenticator Management
compensating_control_1.description:
Mechanisms exist to:
(1) Securely manage authenticators for users and devices; and
(2) Ensure the strength of authentication is appropriate to the classification of the data being accessed.
compensating_control_1.justification: Authenticator Management (IAC-10) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Password Managers (IAC-10.11) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Account Lockout
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to enforce a limit for consecutive invalid login attempts by a user during an organization-defined time period and automatically locks the account when the maximum number of unsuccessful attempts is exceeded.
compensating_control_2.justification: Account Lockout (IAC-22) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Password Managers (IAC-10.11) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
